channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 5.2 magnitude earthquake strikes near San Diego
item.description: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 5.2 rattled the San Diego County area Monday. According to the U.S. Geological Survey, the quake struck at 10:08 a.m. The epicenter was about 2.5 miles South of Julian, at a depth of roughly 8 miles.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: China's Xi Jinping says there are no winners in a tariff war as he visits Southeast Asia
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — China's leader Xi Jinping said no one wins in a trade war as he kicked off a diplomatic tour of Southeast Asia on Monday, presenting China as a force for stability in contrast with U.S.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU and ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former Colorado deputy gets 3 years in prison for fatally shooting man who called for help
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted of killing a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help was sentenced Monday to three years in prison, the maximum sentence allowed, by a judge who said the shooting was about power.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pennsylvania fire chief credits closed doors for saving lives during governor's mansion attack
item.description: Harrisburg Fire Chief Brian Enterline discussed difficulties accessing the Pennsylvania governor's mansion during the fire and what likely saved lives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants Congress to end daylight saving time
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday urged Congress to “push hard for more Daylight at the end of a day” in his latest dig at the semiannual changing of clocks.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspect in arson at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro's residence being treated at hospital, police say
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man who authorities said scaled an iron security fence in the middle of the night, eluded police and broke into the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion where he set a fire is in police custody at a hospital after an unrelated medical event, state police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY AND MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sanders jokingly calls Ocasio-Cortez his daughter at Utah rally
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) joked during a joint appearance at a Utah rally on Sunday that Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) was his daughter, praising the progressive lawmaker and highlighting her background.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: 2022 NCAA woman of the year, family members killed in upstate New York plane crash
item.description: A private plane that crashed in upstate New York over the weekend was carrying six members of a close-knit family of physicians and distinguished student-athletes on a trip to the Catskills for a birthday celebration and the Passover holiday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US northern border faces Great Lakes threat from ‘bad actors’ pressing waterfront weaknesses: expert
item.description: An expert predicted that amid the White House's crackdown on illegal immigration on the southern border, bad actors will naturally turn to the northern border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro and the alleged arson at his official residence
item.description: A man is facing charges after authorities say he broke into the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion in the middle of the night, set a fire that left significant damage and forced Gov. Josh Shapiro, his family and guests to evacuate the building during the Jewish holiday of Passover.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump is 'fully fit' to serve as commander in chief, his doctor says after recent physical
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump’s doctor says the oldest man elected president is “fully fit” to serve as commander in chief as the White House released results Sunday of Trump's recent physical exam.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspect arrested in arson fire that forced Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro, family to flee residence
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man scaled an iron security fence in the middle of the night, eluded police and broke into the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion where he set a fire that left significant damage and forced Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Palou finally loses as IndyCar champ's 2-race winning streak snapped by Kirkwood in Long Beach
item.description: Kyle Kirkwood showed that Alex Palou can be beat this IndyCar season and returned Andretti Global to the top of the podium with his second career victory at the Grand Prix of Long Beach.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 6 injured after explosion, house collapse in Austin
item.description: One house was "leveled" while another "suffered severe collapse damage" following an explosion in Austin on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, KRON4

item.title: 3 cases of rare brain disease reported in Oregon
item.description: Three cases of a rare brain disease have been reported by public health officials in Hood River County.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Flanigan, WGN 9

item.title: Wedding travel: How to minimize costs to make it work
item.description: Nearly 1 in 10 Americans are going to a wedding this summer that requires at least one night away from home.
item.dc:creator: Erin El Issa, NewsNation

item.title: Bernie Sanders, Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez speak at University of Utah
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders will be joined by Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez to speak at the University of Utah on Sunday as part of Sanders' "Fighting Oligarchy" tour.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Search continues for Las Vegas veterinarian who apologized for kicking horse
item.description: Earlier this month, Dr. Frehner apologized for kicking a horse he was hired to give shots to. Days later, he was reported missing in the Lake Mead Recreation Area.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, Fox 5

item.title: Fire at Pennsylvania Governor's Residence was 'act of arson': State Police
item.description: Police said Governor Josh Shapiro and his family were able to safely evacuate after a fire — believed to be an "act of arson" — broke out at the Harrisburg mansion.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Arizona recreation area closed after hiker death and nearly 3 dozen rescues in just 2 days
item.description: Arizona’s Lake Mead National Recreation Area have closed multiple trails due to dangerously high temperatures and the death of one hiker and rescue of 33 others over two days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man wrongly accused in Chiefs parade shooting found dead
item.description: An Olathe, Kansas man who was wrongly accused of being a "shooter" and "illegal alien" at the Chiefs Super Bowl rally in 2024 has died.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Popular soup, bowl products may be contaminated; USDA issues public health alert
item.description: The USDA has issued a nationwide public health alert warning consumers that some popular soup and bowl products could be contaminated with wood.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, NewsNation

item.title: LDS Church loses lawsuit over payment of sexual abuse settlements
item.description: The Utah District Court has ruled against the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints in a lawsuit filed over the payment of sex abuse settlements.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Judge relaxes ban on DOGE access to sensitive US Treasury information
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York judge has relaxed a ban she’d put on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency preventing it from accessing sensitive Treasury Department information related to millions of Americans. Judge Jeannette A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Popular soup, bowl products may be contaminated; USDA issues public health alert
item.description: The USDA has issued a nationwide public health alert warning consumers that some popular soup and bowl products could be contaminated with wood.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WGN 9

item.title: A bucket of beans and someone's pet turtle: Uber reveals 'most unique' items passengers left behind
item.description: It's no surprise that phones, wallets and keys are often left behind in rideshare vehicles. But a urinal or a taxidermied rabbit? Those are slightly more rare.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about activist Mahmoud Khalil and his attorneys' plan to appeal his deportation ruling
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — An immigration judge has ruled that a Palestinian Columbia University graduate student who participated in protests against Israel can be deported. Mahmoud Khalil's attorneys said they will appeal Friday's ruling.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump admin adds migrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The request from Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem to place the migrants on the “Ineligible Master File” maintained by the SSA would bar migrants from receiving benefits or being able to work in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Judge rules Menendez brothers’ bid for freedom through resentencing can continue
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Erik and Lyle Menendez’s resentencing hearings can continue despite opposition from the Los Angeles County district attorney, a judge ruled Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump undergoes his annual physical after years of reluctance to share medical information
item.description: Donald Trump had his annual physical on Friday, a check-up that may give the public its first details in years about the health of a man who, in January, became the oldest in U.S. history to be sworn in as president.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: South Carolina executes second man by firing squad in 5 weeks
item.description: A firing squad on Friday executed a South Carolina man who killed an off-duty police officer, the second time the rare execution method has been used by the state in the past five weeks.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump eyes major cuts to NOAA research
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing cuts to climate, weather and ocean research in a draft budget blueprint for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration adds immigrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The Trump administration directed the Social Security Administration to place more than 6,300 migrants on its list for tracking dead people, blocking their ability to work in a move it hopes will encourage them to “self-deport.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Gas explosion causes $3 million in damages at Spanish Fork dental office
item.description: A gas explosion caused an estimated $3 million in damages at a Spanish Fork dental office Friday morning, according to Spanish Fork Fire & EMS.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: WorldPride organizers caution transgender visitors from abroad
item.description: (The Hill) - Organizers of WorldPride, a global LGBTQ Pride event series to be held this year in Washington, issued an advisory Friday for transgender people from overseas, citing recent policies put forward by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: Here's a look at recent aircraft tragedies, mishaps and close calls
item.description: Six people were killed Thursday in the latest string of aviation disasters after a sightseeing helicopter broke apart and crashed into the Hudson River between New York City and New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
